title: Austrostipa eremophila
intro: Austrostipa eremophila, commonly known as sand grass, is a perennial grass native to Australia, characterized by its slender stems and delicate leaves. The health benefits of Austrostipa eremophila include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which have been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Austrostipa eremophila is commonly used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, to support respiratory health and alleviate symptoms associated with colds and flu.
taxonomy: Austrostipa eremophila is classified within the family Poaceae, commonly known as the grass family. The genus Austrostipa belongs to the subfamily Danthonioideae.
